licenseReview.limit: Catalog, website, worker, and dependency notices remain outside this Skill-body review.

reviews.architecture: two materially overlapping loop-design Skill bodies plus live catalog, publication, website, worker, and catalog infrastructure
reviews.executableSurfaceState: 18 executable files outside the two bodies; unexecuted and excluded from Skill-body comparison
reviews.securityState: body includes untrusted-input treatment, bounded stopping, evidence receipts, and explicit approval for consequential or external actions; live catalog, project LOOPS.md writes, and publication remain separate data and authority boundaries
reviews.portabilityState: core loop body is mostly Agent-neutral, while find and publish depend on an external live catalog and publication surface
reviews.overlapState: strong P20/P24 and adaptive-feedback relevance, but high overlap with current Harness, intake, closure, and authority controls; superiority and net benefit are unproven
reviews.nextUse: compare the loopy body against native reasoning and existing Harness behavior on bounded feedback-loop tasks without executing or publishing

licenseReview.limit: Per-component provenance and any copied examples still require review before redistribution.

reviews.architecture: 17 interdependent role-oriented Skills covering orchestration, phase control, assumptions, dependency impact, tool routing, verification, tone, and memory
reviews.securityState: no executable supply-chain surface in the reviewed tree; process risk remains because several components can block or redirect work and prescribe multi-role handoffs
reviews.portabilityState: mostly prose-based and Agent-neutral, but the tree has dense internal dependencies and several Unity-specific components
reviews.overlapState: very high overlap with intent-contract, capability-router, closure-contract, workspace continuation, memory boundaries, and the current Harness; whole-tree use risks excessive structure on open or divergent work
reviews.nextUse: review an individual component only when a concrete shortfall remains after native and current-chain comparison

licenseReview.limit: Service APIs, SDKs, models, CLIs, and per-component dependencies retain independent terms and data boundaries.

reviews.architecture: 24 domain integration Skills spanning databases, research, delegated agents, media, messaging, project systems, and Google Workspace
reviews.executableSurfaceState: 56 executable files; unexecuted and reviewable only per selected component
reviews.hookState: no repository-level Agent Hook surface detected, although individual integrations can participate in host Hook workflows
reviews.securityState: components introduce API keys, OAuth, persistent authentication, paid external research, account data, uploads, sends, writes, renames, moves, and trash operations; every component has a distinct trust and side-effect boundary
reviews.portabilityState: portable Skill layout is claimed across multiple Agents, but runtime, credential, OS, account, and service behavior varies per component
reviews.overlapState: large overlap with official or runtime-owned Google, messaging, research, database, and account-connected capabilities; no generic human-AI Harness gap is proven by suite breadth
reviews.nextUse: select one component only after a concrete task proves current native, official, installed, or already-authorized capabilities insufficient

licenseReview.limit: Node dependencies, provider SDKs, copied project examples, and generated host variants remain independently reviewable.

reviews.architecture: five distinct Skill bodies represented by nine host paths plus auto-activation, mandatory-skill guard, edit tracking, session update, provider, embedding, vector-store, build, and setup infrastructure
reviews.hookState: 39 Agent Hook files; independent Hook security and host-semantics review required
reviews.securityState: optional external LLM keys, prompt injection, write blocking, file-change tracking, session persistence, dependency installation, and automatic context injection expand the trust and failure surface
reviews.portabilityState: primarily Claude Code and bash/WSL oriented; duplicate host copies do not prove behavioral parity
reviews.overlapState: auto-routing, mandatory activation, verification, continuation, and Skill creation overlap the current chain; mandatory edit blocking conflicts with open and assist postures unless independently justified
reviews.nextUse: compare dynamic activation and observability ideas only; do not treat duplicate Skill paths or Hook count as maturity

licenseReview.limit: Not a permissive open-source license; redistribution, service, notice, modification, and license-key conditions require a separate legal and release review.

reviews.architecture: MCP server, sandbox execution and indexing tools, local SQLite session continuity, eleven Skill paths, broad cross-host adapters, automatic injection, routing enforcement, diagnostics, upgrade, purge, and hosted Insight entry points
reviews.hookState: 95 Agent Hook files across many hosts; independent Hook, MCP, storage, update, and adapter review required
reviews.securityState: claims local-only storage and credential redaction, while Hooks capture tool events, user prompts, rules, file edits, git operations, tasks, errors, and session state into local databases; interception, persistence, deletion, debug export, update, and hosted dashboard boundaries require behavioral validation
reviews.portabilityState: broad host coverage is claimed and implemented through host-specific adapters; parity and current host contracts remain unverified
reviews.overlapState: strong P5/P6/P8/P21/P24 relevance, but mandatory rerouting of most commands and always-enable-Hook guidance conflict with the project's open/assist/guarded and minimal-sufficiency principles unless real net benefit is demonstrated
reviews.nextUse: separate context-reduction value testing from license, privacy, storage, MCP, Hook, update, and over-control review

batchDecision.nextGate: demand-level native and current-chain comparison, then component-specific security portability overlap redundancy and behavior evidence for only the selected marginal candidates

recheckTriggers: a pinned revision, license, source structure, Hook surface, dependency, account boundary, or maintainer state changes
a concrete demand selects one component for deeper review
a download, execution, installation, account connection, Hook enablement, adaptation, admission, release, or standardization action is proposed
real multi-Agent and multi-task evidence changes the current overlap or net-benefit hypothesis
